methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Matic Robotics is a seed-stage consumer technology company founded by Navneet Dalal and Mehul Nariyawala that has raised $107M to date. The comparable universe for this analysis is limited, consisting of one identifiable private peer (Vital Lyfe) and no public comparables in the consumer tech robotics space.

The stage-progression method is the applicable valuation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($107M) as a proxy for base valuation, the analysis derives an implied $6M base value and applies a typical seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x, yielding an implied valuation of $16M. This represents the core quantitative output of the triangulation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M (low) to $19M (median $16M), with this range reflecting the single method applied and inherent uncertainty in stage-progression assumptions. Key limitations include the sparse comparable universe, reliance on a single valuation method, and the absence of public market benchmarks for validation. The step-up multiplier itself carries material sensitivity—variations in that assumption would meaningfully shift results. Given the early stage and capital intensity of the sector, investors should weight this analysis as directional rather than definitive, and prioritize primary diligence on product-market fit and burn dynamics.
